sn24.9:1.1: At Sāvatthī. "Bhikkhus, when what exists, by clinging to what, by adhering to what, does such a view as this arise: ‘The world is eternal′?"[n.263] The next ten suttas are each devoted to one of the ten "undeclared points," also dealt with from still different angles in SN 33 and SN 44.
sn24.9:1.2: "Venerable sir, our teachings are rooted in the Blessed One … ."
sn24.9:4.1: "When, bhikkhus, a noble disciple has abandoned perplexity in these six cases … he is then called a noble disciple who is a stream-enterer … with enlightenment as his destination."
